name: Embalse de Beleña – circular from Cogoll
name_original: Embalse de Beleña – circular desde Cogolludo por la laguna de Tamajón
description: This challenging 36.1-mile mountain bike route from Cogolludo explores the Embalse de Beleña and Laguna de Tamajón with 3393 feet of gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cogolludo offers a diverse range of terrain within the Sierra Norte region of Guadalajara, Spain. The area is characterized by a mountainous landscape with valleys, featuring holm oak and pine groves. Riders can expect varied trails that navigate around natural features like the Beleña Reservoir and unique limestone formations. This region provides a compelling mix of natural beauty and varied terrain suitable for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Cogolludo The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de Beleña – circular from Cogoll, a 36.1 miles (58.1 km) difficult trail that takes about 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and explores the Beleña Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Beleña Reservoir – La Mierla MTB Trail 9 loop from Humanes de Mohernando, a moderate 28.9 miles (46.5 km) path. This loop offers varied terrain around the reservoir and takes approximately 3 hours 50 minutes. Local mountain bikers also love the Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval, a 13.6 miles (21.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 51 minutes. This easy route is suitable for less experienced riders. Mountain biking around Cogolludo is defined by mountainous landscapes, diverse trails, and unique geological formations including reservoirs and limestone features. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more advanced routes. The routes in Cogolludo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 10 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cogolludo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cogolludo?
A: There are over 40 mountain bike trails around Cogolludo, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 5 easy routes, 28 moderate routes, and 8 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Cogolludo?
A: Yes, Cogolludo offers 5 easy mountain bike routes. A good option for less experienced riders is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006154663/los-huertos-tamajon-circular-a-las-ruinas-del-monasterio-bonaval" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Huertos - Tamajón – circular a las ruinas del Monasterio Bonaval&lt;/a&gt;, which is 21.9 km long with 260m of elevation gain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cogolludo?
A: The terrain around Cogolludo is diverse, characterized by mountainous landscapes, valleys, holm oak and pine groves. You'll encounter varied paths that navigate around natural features like the Beleña Reservoir and unique limestone formations. Some routes may include steep sections requiring good fitness.

Q: What are some scenic points or natural features to look out for on the trails?
A: Many routes incorporate the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7464158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de La Puebla de Beleña&lt;/a&gt; and the Beleña Reservoir. The region is also known for unique geological formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5141678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City of Tamajón&lt;/a&gt;, with its caves, rock arches, and sinkholes, which can be explored nearby.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, there are 8 difficult routes for experienced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004676978/embalse-de-belena-circular-from-cogoll" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Beleña – circular from Cogoll&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 58.1 km trail with over 1,000m of elevation gain, exploring the Beleña Reservoir.

Q: What historical landmarks can be found near the mountain bike trails?
A: You can find several historical landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3944455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bonaval Monastery Ruins&lt;/a&gt; are accessible via some routes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Los Enebrales&lt;/a&gt; is another notable site. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3947774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Bridge over the Jarama River&lt;/a&gt; offers great views and access to nearby caves.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Cogolludo?
A: The mountain bike trails around Cogolludo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful natural scenery, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking in Cogolludo?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails around Cogolludo are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14886397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beleña Reservoir – La Mierla MTB Trail 9 loop from Humanes de Mohernando&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 46.5 km loop offering varied terrain around the reservoir.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails in Cogolludo?
A: While specific parking areas for each trail are not detailed, Cogolludo is a town with general parking facilities. It is advisable to check local parking options in Cogolludo or near the start points of your chosen routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cogolludo?
A: The Sierra Norte region, where Cogolludo is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring colder weather, though trails may still be accessible depending on conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or bodies of water?
A: Yes, several routes feature the region's reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Valdesotos – Vado Reservoir loop from Puebla de Beleña&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 48.8 km route that includes the Vado Reservoir.
tips: Having a beer with a skewer here is a delight 😋.
The Tamajon lagoon has a nice picnic area and a small shelter with a roof, perfect for overnight stays.

The Beleña reservoir built on the Sorbe river offers a very picturesque landscape of this area of Guadalajara. From the top of the dam you can have beautiful views of the reservoir with the Ocejón in the background, which rises to 2049 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and is not suitable for beginners. It demands a very good physical condition and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for challenging sections where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse and beautiful landscapes. You'll pass by the Beleña Reservoir and the Laguna Tamajón. The area is part of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park, featuring mountains, valleys, and varied ecosystems like juniper, sabina, and holm oak forests. You might also encounter distinctive limestone rock formations near the 'Ciudad Encantada de Tamajón'.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and can be demanding. While specific surface details aren't provided, the route includes diverse paths, some of which are likely unpaved and challenging for cycling, consistent with a 'difficult' mountain biking classification.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Cogolludo. A good reference point in Cogolludo is the Plaza Duques de Medinaceli, where you can likely find parking to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given its location in the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking this route. The weather is typically milder, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and the colder conditions of winter, which can make the challenging terrain even more difficult.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park generally allows dogs. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users, especially given the challenging nature of this mountain biking route.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or specific permits required for general access to the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park for activities like mountain biking. However, always check local regulations for any specific restrictions or protected areas within the park that might require special permission.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino a Fraguas, Camino de Cogolludo, Camino Peña del Ciervo, Pista Monasterio- Veguillas, Calle de los caballitos, Camino de la Salceda, Camino de Jócar, and Carretera Arbancón-Monasterio.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the trail in Cogolludo or Tamajón?
faqs.answer: Both Cogolludo and Tamajón are small towns that serve as gateways to the natural park. You can find local cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in these villages, which are convenient for refueling or resting before or after your challenging ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the route's 'difficult' rating and length (58.1 km), you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, appropriate navigation tools (like the komoot app), and weather-appropriate clothing. A helmet is essential, and consider knee/elbow pads for the technical sections.

faqs.question: Is there an insider tip for tackling this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Since this is a demanding circular route, consider starting early in the day, especially during warmer months, to allow ample time for completion (estimated over 5 hours) and to avoid the midday heat. Pacing yourself and being prepared to dismount and push your bike on particularly steep or technical sections will make the experience more manageable and enjoyable.
